titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/95

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=385 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:25:42.274433+00 | translation moa=386 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:33:32.714656+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis . tam presentibus quam futuris . Willelmus de Morev' Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et hac presenti mea car
ta confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam
libertatem et licentiam arandi et seminandi totam terram de bleyneslei . et totam planitiem in circuitu extra nemus usque ad ledre
et in faciendi commodum suum secundum uoluntatem suam usque infra diuisas suas excepto sarto faciendo usque ad Windeslawe
et inde per magnam stratam que descendit per Windeslawe uersus laweder usque ad diuisas de laweder . sicut pater meus Ric'
de Moreuill' illis in liberam elemosinam dedit. Hanc autem predictam libertate et licentiam illis concessi peticione patris mei .
et pro salute animarum dauid et Melcolmi quondam Regum scott' dominorum meorum . et pro salute Willelmi Regis Scott' . domini mei et pro salute
anime mee et anime uxoris mee et omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . Quare uolo ut predicti Monachi prenominatam liber
tatem et licentiam ita libere et quiete habeant in perpetuam elemosinam et possideant . sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius et quietius et
honorificentius possident . His testibus . Iocelino GlasG' episcopo . Ric' de Morv' patre meo . Auice de loncastre Matre mea herber
to decano GlasG' . Clemente medico capellano . Adam persona ecclesie de laweder . Rogero clerico dapifero patris mei . Alano de
Thirlestan . Willelmo Mansello . fintor [?for funtore or finitore]. Herberto filio Roberti . Alano de Clalpeham .

To all sons of holy Mother Church, both present and to come, William de Morville sends greeting. Know me to have granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the freedom and licence to plough and sow the whole land of Blainslie, and the whole plain around the outside of the wood down to the Leader, and to make good use of it according to their will between its boundaries, except for clearing woodland, as far as 'Windeslawe' and from there by the great road which descends by 'Windeslawe' towards Lauder, down to the boundaries of Lauder, just as my father Richard de Morville gave them in free alms. This foresaid liberty and licence I have granted to them at the request of my father, and for the salvation of the souls of David and Malcolm, late kings of the Scots, my lords, and for the salvation of William, king of the Scots, my lord, and for the salvation of my own soul, and of the soul of my wife and of all my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I will that the foresaid monks should have and possess the previously named liberty and licence as freely and quitely in everlasting alms, just as they possess any alms freely and quitely and honourably. With these witnesses: Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Richard de Morville, my father; Avice of Lancaster, my mother; Herbert, dean of Glasgow; Clement the physician, chaplain; Adam, parson of the church of Lauder; Roger the clerk, my father's steward; Alan of Thirlestane; William Mansell, fintor [?founder or ?surveyor of boundaries*]; Herbert son of Robert; Alan of Clapham.
*fintor is not attested; finitor is otherwise only attested in Classical Latin; funtor, 'founder', is the most likely reading.
